entry.title: Re: WE02 List Specific Segment – How to Display/Export Pa...
entry.content: Understood. Is there a standard SAP transaction that can display a specific segment (for example, Z1DCSCONSDET) across multiple ZDCSCONS IDocs and include the parent IDoc number for each segment record? I'm looking for a way to export the detail segment data while retaining the corresponding IDoc number or creation timestamp.

entry.title: Difference between SAC Enterprise and SAC Embedded (Known limitations, restrictions)
entry.content:
1. Use case 1: The user wants to know if there're limitations and restrictions when using SAC Embedded Analytics Stories. 
  2. Use case 2: The user wants to know if the feature "Share > Publish to catalog" in SAC Enterprise, enables the consumption of the the SAC Enterprise Story inside S/4HANA. And does it consume a license in SAC Enterprise or no additional fee (as it is embedded in S/4HANA as a native feature)

entry.title: Re: Working with SAP BTP SDK for Android and older Android devices with DigiCert G5 Certificate
entry.content:
Hello,

Thanks for the write-up! Small inconsistency in Step 1: the download link points to the ECC G5 root (DigiCertTLSECCP384RootG5.crt.pem), but the filename and config reference below use the RSA name (digicert_tls_rsa_4096_root_g5) — and SAP Note 3708420 (https://me.sap.com/notes/3708420) links the RSA root instead.

In my testing, every G5-migrated BTP host chained to DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5; I didn't see the ECC root used anywhere. Which is correct to bundle RSA, ECC, or both? And is the ECC link a typo?

Thanks!

entry.title: Putaway bin proposal for multiple fixed bins
entry.content:
During the fixed bin putaway scenario, if a product has multiple fixed bins assigned, our standard understanding is that the system proposes the bin that comes first based on the sort sequence.

However, our requirement is that when multiple fixed bins are assigned to a particular product, the bin that was added first in the /scwm/binmat should be proposed for putaway, instead of the bin determined by the sort sequence.

Kindly confirm whether this behavior is supported in standard functionality. Also, please let us know if there is any BAdI or enhancement option available to achieve this requirement.


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

  • Check the sort sequence maintained for the fixed bins in storage type X.
  • In /SCWM/BINMAT, assign multiple fixed bins to the same product for the particular st type.
  • Ensure the fixed bins are created at different times so that each assignment has a different creation timestamp. In a test case, BIN6 was assigned first, followed by BIN2, BIN4, etc.
  • Create the inbound delivery for the product.
  • Create the putaway Warehouse Task (WT) for the inbound delivery.Observe the destination bin proposed by the system:
  • Actual Result: The system proposes BIN2, which has the smallest sort sequence among the assigned fixed bins.
  • Expected Result: The system should propose BIN6, as it was the first fixed bin assigned to the product in /SCWM/BINMAT, regardless of the sort sequence.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Putaway bin proposal for multiple fixed bins
entry.content:
Hello,

Standard EWM does not propose a destination bin based on creation timestamp in /SCWM/BINMAT.

If the standard behavior is not enough for your business processes, the following BAdI from Enhancement Spot /SCWM/ES_CORE_PTS can be used to implement custom bin sorting logic:

/SCWM/EX_CORE_PTS_FILT_SORT - Filter and Sort Possible Storage Bins
An example implementation is available in the system under class /SCWM/CL_EI_CORE_PTS_FILT_SORT.

Regards,
SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Re: Limitations with Business Rule - Benefit Employee Claim SuccessFactors
entry.content: Hey Bijo , Thank for replying back.Yes , Support ticket was already raised and it was ruled out as limitation with the product.I am happy to connect with you , Sharing the details over DM!

entry.title: Re: How to use MASS OUTPUT button in FWO list?
entry.content: BTW, i just want to send out emails. And i dont want to use external communication. Instead of external communication, if i use Method Call then this PPF is not visible in Mass Output.

entry.title: 信用管理咨询：在未超过信用总额度的情况下，如何基于客户“超期未清项”自动拦截交货单？
entry.content:
Document must be blocked for above 100,000.00 CNY.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 信用管理咨询：在未超过信用总额度的情况下，如何基于客户“超期未清项”自动拦截交货单？
entry.content: Implement Check for Maximum Document Value. Check the available check steps documentation. link

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors - Assigned Learning Items Not Showing in For You Today
entry.content:
We have note enabled the New Home Page upon updating to 1H 2026, however, we have observed behavior that seems different as it relates to the "For You Today" information being displayed about user Learning assignments. 

Previously, users would see any assigned training in "For You Today" once it was assigned.   We recently noticed that under our current configuration (using legacy home page with 1H 2026), "For You Today" does not display a tile for any outstanding Learning assignments...seemingly not until the assignment is 7 days from being due. 

The concern is that with the deployment of 1H 2026, something about For You Today defaulted to some setting that we need to adjust, but it's unclear based on what documentation I've found where exactly that is.  

One possible solution is to adjust the Learning plan item visibility threshold under Learning Administration > System Administration > Configuration > System Configuration > USER LEARNING PLAN as described here.  But I've yet to uncover a straightforward solution to the specific problem.

entry.title: Automating Creation of Down Payment Request without the use of Billing Plan
entry.content:
Aside from using a billing plan, is there a standard SAP approach to automatically create a customer down payment request from a sales order based on the assigned payment term?

We plan to introduce a payment term that requires customers to pay 100% of the sales order value upfront. This payment term will be assigned to most customers. However, the current process requires users to manually create the customer down payment request for each sales order, which is time-consuming and inefficient.

We are looking for a way to automatically generate the customer down payment request when a sales order with this payment term is created, without relying on billing plans. Is there a standard SAP functionality, enhancement, or recommended approach to achieve this?

entry.title: Re: SAP DM 2608 Release Notes Missing – Has Anyone Heard...
entry.content:
Hi @dewolfsl_ifm, a preliminary draft version has been published, and is accessible for logged (S) users, check here. 

The final version is still pending, and should be published soon.

You can also check Note #3764341 - Additional Information and Manual Activities for SAP Digital Manufacturing, Release 2608

Hope it helps! Br, Manoel

entry.title: Re: Adding a Custom Column in Fiori Elements (List Report + Object Page)
entry.content: Thanks for this blog

entry.title: Introducing the SAP Activate Prompt Library: Curated AI Prompts for Your Implementation Project
entry.content:
One of the most common questions we hear from implementation consultants who are getting started with AI is: "Where do I begin? What exactly do I type?"

It is a fair question. The value of a tool like SAP Joule is immense — but only if you know how to prompt it effectively. Writing a good prompt for a complex, context-rich task like preparing fit-to-standard workshops, drafting a project charter, or building a data migration approach takes time and expertise. And without a starting point, many teams either default to generic, underpowered prompts or simply do not use the capability at all.

Today, we are solving that problem. We are excited to introduce the SAP Activate Prompt Library — a centralized, curated collection of implementation-ready prompts, available directly on SAP for Me.

 

What Is the SAP Activate Prompt Library?

The SAP Activate Prompt Library  is a portal hosted within the SAP Activate road map viewer  on SAP for Me. It provides a structured, searchable collection of prompts crafted specifically for SAP implementation project tasks, aligned to the SAP Activate methodology.

Every prompt in the library has been developed, tested, and validated by the Experts and Architects. They are not generic AI prompts — they are precision-engineered instructions that put Joule into the exact role and context your project team needs, aligned to specific phases, roadmaps, and deliverables.

The prompts are designed for use with SAP Joule, the AI assistant purpose-built for SAP implementation professionals. Joule allows your consultants and project team members to interact with an AI that understands the SAP implementation landscape — and the prompts in this library are designed to get the very best out of it.

How to Access the Prompt Library

The Prompt Library is available to all users through the SAP Activate Road Map Viewer on SAP for Me. Navigate to: https://me.sap.com/roadmapviewer/promptlib 

Fig: SAP Activate Prompt Library

No additional setup is required to browse the library. To use the prompts in SAP Joule for Consultants, you will need access to that capability in your SAP environment.

A Walkthrough of the Prompt Library

The Main View: Browse and Filter

The library is fully filterable across four dimensions, making it easy to surface exactly what you need:

  • Roadmap: SAP Activate for SAP Cloud ERP; Private – New Implementation; Private – Selective Data Transition; Private – System Conversion; S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Joule Type: Filter by the specific Joule capability being used
  • Phase: Prepare, Explore, Realize, and others
  • Task: Filter by the type of implementation task

You can also search by keyword and save your most-used prompts to a Favorites list for quick access.

Fig: Filters

 

Opening a Prompt: Three Layers of Value

Clicking on any prompt card opens a detail panel with three key sections.

The Prompt

When you open the Prompt Library, you are presented with a card-based view of all available prompts. Each card shows you:

  • The prompt title — a clear, task-oriented name (e.g., "Create Project Charter and Scope Statement", "Prioritize Delta Requirements")
  • The SAP Activate phase — Prepare, Explore, Realize, etc.
  • The target roadmap — which SAP Activate implementation scenario the prompt applies to
  • The target Joule tool — currently SAP Joule for Consultants, we are expanding rapidly
  • A preview of the prompt context — so you can quickly assess relevance without opening the card

Fig: Prompt Selection

 This structure ensures the AI response is grounded, specific, and actionable — not generic. You can view the prompt in the detail panel or click Expand for a full-screen view. A Copy button lets you instantly copy the prompt text to paste into Joule for Consultants.

Fig: Expand Prompts

Expert Template (where applicable)

Some prompts in the library are designed to work with Experts in SAP Joule for Consultants. Experts are custom AI personas that are configured in Joule for Consultants with a specific role, expertise, and behavior set — essentially giving Joule a specialized identity for a particular type of work.

For these prompts, the library includes the full Expert Template: the system-level instructions you paste into Joule for Consultants to create or configure the Expert. For example, the "Create Project Charter and Scope Statement" prompt is designed for a Project Manager Expert — an Expert persona with 15+ years of SAP implementation experience, trained to think in terms of project risks, dependencies, and structured governance deliverables.

The Expert Template is available directly in the prompt detail panel with a single Copy Instructions button — making it fast to set up the Expert and start working.

Fig: Expand Experts

Sample Output

One of the most valuable features of the Prompt Library is the included Sample Output for each prompt. This is a real example of what a well-formed response from Joule looks like when the prompt is used correctly — complete with the expected document structure, depth of content, and formatting.

Sample outputs serve multiple purposes: they help you calibrate your expectations, validate that your Joule session is producing quality results, and can serve as reference examples for stakeholders who are new to AI-assisted delivery.

Fig: Expand Sample Output

What's Next

The SAP Activate Prompt Library is a living resource. The SAP Activate team will continue expanding the library with new prompts across additional phases, roadmaps, and task areas — aligned with the ongoing evolution of SAP Joule for Consultants and the broader SAP Business AI capabilities.

We are also expanding the coverage of Expert Templates, as new expert personas are developed and validated for implementation scenarios beyond project management — including solution architects, functional consultants, data architects, and more.

The library is one part of SAP Activate's broader commitment to Dimension 3 of Business AI: leveraging AI capabilities throughout the implementation lifecycle to accelerate delivery, improve quality, and empower your project team.

Get Started Today

The SAP Activate Prompt Library is available now at https://me.sap.com/roadmapviewer/promptlib.

Browse the prompts, explore the Expert Templates, and try them in your next project task. We would love to hear how you are using the library and what additional prompts would be most valuable to you. Please share your feedback in the comments below or reach out to us at sapactivatemethodology@sap.com.

entry.title: SAP Event Mesh for Beginners – Part 3: Push Delivery with Webhooks
entry.content:
SAP Event Mesh for Beginners – Part 3: Push Delivery with Webhooks

Part 3 of the SAP Event Mesh beginner series.

A quick recap

By the end of Part 2, you had events publishing into Event Mesh over HTTP and CPI consuming them from a queue.

Part 1 (this post):  EDA concepts + provisioning the Event Mesh instance, queues, subscriptions, and service key (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sap-event-mesh-for-beginners-part-1-event-driven-architecture-topics-queues/ba-p/14428493)

Part 2: Testing with Postman + CPI iFlow configuration (publish & consume) (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sap-event-mesh-for-beginners-part-2-testing-with-postman-amp-cpi-iflow/ba-p/14428855)

Part 3: Push Delivery with Webhooks 
Part 4:  Common errors, fixes, and a quick-reference cheat sheet

But there's a catch with everything we've done so far. Every consumption approach in Part 2 was pull-based — your CPI iFlow (AMQP Sender, or the REST /consumption call) reaches out to the queue and asks "any messages for me?" That works, but it means CPI is doing the polling, and there's always a small delay.

There's a second delivery model that flips this around: the webhook. Instead of CPI pulling, Event Mesh pushes — the moment a message lands in the queue, Event Mesh calls your endpoint. No polling, near real-time, and it's the pattern most S/4HANA → CPI integrations actually use in the wild.

If you went looking for "webhook" on the Queue screen and couldn't find it — you're not missing anything. It lives somewhere else, and we'll get there.

 

1. Pull vs Push — when to use which

Both models start the same way: a queue subscribed to your topics collects messages. The difference is who initiates delivery.

  Pull (Part 2) Push (this post)
Who initiatesCPI reaches out to the queueEvent Mesh calls your endpoint
CPI adapterAMQP Sender / REST consumptionHTTPS Sender (receives the call)
TimingPolling intervalNear real-time
Best forBatch-style / CPI-driven pacingEvent-driven, low-latency S/4HANA → CPI

A webhook subscription connects your webhook to a queue, and when messages reach that queue, it delivers them to your webhook in near real time. Because there's no polling, it consumes fewer resources on both sides.

For most "react to an S/4HANA business event" scenarios, push (webhook) is the right choice.

2. Build the receiving iFlow first

A webhook needs somewhere to push to — an HTTPS endpoint that's ready and waiting. In CPI, that's an iFlow with an HTTP Sender adapter. Its address becomes your webhook URL.

  1. Create a new iFlow (e.g. EventMesh_Webhook_Receiver).
  2. On the Sender → HTTPS adapter:
    • Address: something like /eventmesh-webhook → the full URL becomes https://<your-cpi-tenant>/http/eventmesh-webhook
    • Authorization: User Role, role ESBMessaging.send (this is what the webhook's OAuth token will need permission for).
  3. Add whatever processing you need after the sender (map the CloudEvent, call an OData API, etc.).
  4. Deploy the iFlow — the endpoint must be live before you create the webhook, or the handshake (next section) will fail.

 

✅Tip: Copy the deployed endpoint URL from Monitor → Manage Integration Content → your iFlow → Endpoints. That exact URL is what you'll paste as the webhook URL.

3. Get the credential that lets the webhook call into CPI

Here's the single most important thing to understand about webhooks, and where almost everyone gets confused:

⚠️The webhook authenticates INTO CPI — so it uses CPI's inbound credential, not the Event Mesh service key.

Everything up to now used the Event Mesh key (so CPI could talk to Event Mesh). A webhook is the opposite direction: Event Mesh is now the caller, and it needs credentials to get into your CPI endpoint.

Those credentials come from a Process Integration Runtime service instance:

  1. In your CPI subaccount → Instances and Subscriptions → Create → service Process Integration Runtime, plan integration-flow.
  2. Add the role/grant for ESBMessaging.send (API access).
  3. Create a Service Key on it → note the clientid, clientsecret, and tokenurl.

These three values are what secure the webhook. Keep them separate in your head from the Event Mesh key — mixing them up is the #1 webhook failure.

4. Create the webhook

Now the part you couldn't find. It's not on the Queue screen — it's on the message client.

  1. In Event Mesh, open the Overview tab and click your Message Client → this opens the Webhooksconfiguration screen → click Create.
  2. Fill in:
Field Value
Namewebhook-3pl (any name)
Queue NameS4HC/Testing/Masterdata/3pl (the queue to attach to)
Webhook URLhttps://<your-cpi-tenant>/http/eventmesh-webhook (the deployed HTTPS Sender endpoint)
Quality of Service1 (recommended — see section 6)
AuthenticationOAuth2 Client Credentials
Client ID / Secret / Token URLfrom the Process Integration Runtime service key (section 3)
  1. Save. On success you'll see the subscription added.

 

From here, any message that lands in the 3pl queue is pushed to your iFlow — no polling, no AMQP Sender needed.

5. The handshake (what it is, why it fails)

Before Event Mesh sends a single real message, it does a handshake to make sure your endpoint actually agreed to receive pushes.

The handshake is an OPTIONS call to your webhook URL carrying a WebHook-Request-Origin header. Your endpoint must reply with a WebHook-Allowed-Origin header whose value matches that origin (or is *). Only after a successful handshake does Event Mesh start publishing messages to the webhook.

The good news: a properly deployed CPI HTTPS Sender endpoint handles this automatically. The usual reasons a handshake fails:

  • The iFlow isn't deployed or the endpoint isn't reachable.
  • The credentials are wrong (remember: CPI runtime key, not Event Mesh key).
  • The endpoint requires an auth the webhook isn't sending.

If the webhook shows as not active or "handshake failed," check those three before anything else.

6. QoS and the retry behavior

When you create the webhook you pick a Quality of Service:

  • QoS 0 (at most once): Event Mesh posts the message to your webhook and deletes it from the queue regardlessof whether your endpoint succeeded. Fast, but you can lose messages if CPI was down.
  • QoS 1 (at least once): Event Mesh posts the message and waits for a 2XX response as acknowledgment. Any 2XX = success, message removed. Anything else = the message stays and gets retried.

That retry behavior is worth knowing: on a non-2XX response the service resends roughly every 15 minutes, gradually slowing the frequency, and keeps redelivering for up to 30 days unless your webhook positively acknowledges. So a broken downstream won't silently drop events — but it also means a misbehaving endpoint can pile up retries.

✅Tip: For anything you can't afford to lose (master data, business events), use QoS 1. You can pause a webhook during maintenance and resume it later without losing queued messages.

7. Troubleshooting

Symptom Likely cause Fix
Handshake failed / webhook not activeiFlow not deployed, or endpoint unreachableDeploy the iFlow; confirm the endpoint URL from Monitor → Endpoints
401 on deliveryWrong credential direction — used the Event Mesh keyUse the Process Integration Runtime client id/secret/token URL
403 on deliveryOAuth client lacks the API roleGrant ESBMessaging.send to the CPI runtime instance
Messages pile up in the queue, never deliveredEndpoint returns non-2XX (QoS 1 keeps retrying)Fix the receiving iFlow so it returns 2XX; check CPI monitoring
Nothing arrives at allQueue isn't subscribed to the right topic (case-sensitive!)Re-check the queue's topic subscription — same gotcha as the rest of the series

8. Quick Reference

🔒Replace all placeholders with your own values. Never publish real secrets.

Item Value
Where to createEvent Mesh → Overview → Message Client → Webhooks → Create
Webhook URLhttps://<your-cpi-tenant>/http/<iflow-address> (deployed HTTPS Sender endpoint)
Auth typeOAuth2 Client Credentials
Auth sourceProcess Integration Runtime service key (NOT the Event Mesh key)
Required CPI roleESBMessaging.send
HandshakeOPTIONS + WebHook-Request-Origin → reply WebHook-Allowed-Origin (match or *)
QoS 0delete after post, regardless of result
QoS 1needs 2XX ack; retries ~15 min, backs off, up to 30 days
Maintenancepause / resume the webhook

What's next

Webhooks complete the delivery picture: Part 2 gave you the pull model (CPI reaches out), and this post gives you the push model (Event Mesh reaches in). For real-time S/4HANA → CPI event handling, push is usually what you want.

The one idea to carry away: credential direction. Publishing/consuming uses the Event Mesh key; the webhook uses your CPI runtime key, because the caller and callee have swapped.

In Part 4 — Troubleshooting & Quick Reference, we'll bring the whole series together: every error you're likely to hit across setup, publishing, consuming, and webhooks — with causes, fixes, and a one-page cheat sheet to keep open while you build.

🔒Security note: All client IDs, secrets, tenant URLs, and endpoints in this post are placeholders. Use your own values, keep them out of public posts, and rotate anything that's been exposed.

Primary Tag: SAP Integration Suite Tags: SAP Event Mesh, Cloud Integration, SAP CPI, Webhooks, OAuth2, Event-Driven Architecture, SAP BTP

entry.title: Mastering HR Data in the AI Era: Why Peter Aiken’s Framework matters for successful AI adoption
entry.content:
The AI revolution in HR isn’t coming. It’s here. But it only works for organizations that have done the foundational work. Those that didn’t are discovering that you can’t bolt AI onto broken data foundations.

In the AI age, HR data is no longer just administrative—it’s a strategic asset that powers predictive insights, personalized employee experiences, and competitive advantage. In my other blog: Why HR Data Management Strategy is important in your HR Transformation, I described Peter Aiken’s framework for Data Management and how it relates perfectly with the journey SAP SuccessFactors customers go thru during their HR transformation. Peter Aiken, President of DAMA International and a leading voice in data strategy, provides a proven framework that shifts organizations from reactive data fixes to proactive governance.

Peter Aiken’s Framework: The Five Pillars

SAP's Autonomous HCM reveals why Aiken’s data management capabilities aren’t theoretical luxuries but operational necessities. Joule’s ability to deliver accurate natural language insights, automate workflows, and provide contextual recommendations depends entirely on the data foundation you’ve built.

Joule’s Core Data Requirements Mapped to Aiken’s Framework

1. Technical Infrastructure (Aiken’s Foundation Layer)

Joule demands enterprise-grade technical readiness:

  • SAP SuccessFactors license in supported data centers with proper entitlements
  • Identity Authentication Service (IAS) integration for secure user authentication using OAuth2 or SAML
  • SAP BTP destinations configured properly (LPS_SFSF_rt, HTML5.Dynamic Destination)
  • OData API access to Employee Central portlets (Personal Info, Job Info, Address Info, CompoundEmployee)

Without Aiken’s technical infrastructure capability: These integrations fail, Joule can’t access employee data, and your AI investment produces zero value.

2. Logical Infrastructure (Master Data Relationships)

Joule leverages the CompoundEmployee API to understand how employee records connect across modules:

  • How job information links to compensation data
  • How performance reviews relate to goal progress
  • How organizational hierarchies map to reporting structures

Without Aiken’s logical infrastructure: Joule receives fragmented data and can’t provide holistic insights. For example, it might recommend a promotion without seeing the complete performance history because the relationships weren’t properly mapped.

3. Data Quality (The Make-or-Break Factor)

Joule performs optimally only with:

  • Complete Employee Central data via CompoundEmployee API
  • Standardized portlet data (Job Info, Personal Info, Compensation)
  • Clean metadata definitions via OData/SFAPI Data Dictionaries
  • Audit trails and version control on all HR documents

Incomplete records, inconsistent naming conventions, or missing relationships produce unreliable AI outputs.

Without Aiken’s data quality capability: Joule delivers "garbage in, garbage out." Incomplete Job Info portlets lead to flawed promotion recommendations; missing performance lineage creates unreliable AI agents.

4. Metadata Management (AI Transparency)

Joule relies on documented metadata for accuracy:

  • Document Grounding: Indexed HR documents (contracts, policies, handbooks) for retrieval-augmented generation, preventing AI hallucinations
  • Data Dictionaries: OData and SFAPI dictionaries that define what each data element means
  • Real-time Context: Current role, location, permissions, and performance history to personalize responses

Without Aiken’s metadata management: Joule can’t distinguish between outdated policies and current ones, potentially giving employees incorrect information about benefits or compliance requirements.

5. Governance Guidelines (Trust and Compliance)

Joule inherits your governance framework:

  • Role-based permissions determine what data each employee can query
  • Audit trails track every AI interaction for compliance
  • Data stewardship ensures accountability when AI makes recommendations

Without Aiken’s governance capability: Joule might expose confidential compensation data, violate GDPR, or make recommendations based on unauthorized data access.

SAP SuccessFactors offers the technical platform, but success depends on the data management discipline you bring. When implemented together:

  • Employee records become predictive assets rather than administrative burdens
  • Joule transforms from expensive novelty to trusted advisor
  • HR leaders shift from reactive firefighting to proactive strategy
  • AI delivers measurable ROI instead of joining the 80% failure statistic

Joule isn’t just another HR tool—it’s the convergence point where decades of data management best practices meet cutting-edge AI capabilities. Peter Aiken’s framework provides the exact blueprint for this convergence.

Start with Aiken’s iterative approach:

  1. identify your highest-value AI use case
  2. map the required data dependencies
  3. implement the five capabilities systematically
  4. expand from success.

When you ask Joule about promotion eligibility, workforce planning scenarios, or policy interpretations, you’re not just testing an AI—you’re testing whether your organization has mastered data as a strategic asset. The organizations that invested in Aiken’s capabilities years ago are now reaping AI dividends.

Want to know more about how you can get your data ready to adopt AI? Get in touch with the SAP SuccessFactors HR Innovation & Transformation team. Send a message to saphit@sap.com or reach out to me via email kristine.reyes@sap.com .

entry.title: Re: Silent Failure in SAP Datasphere Replication Flows -...
entry.content: Thank you for confirming and sharing this workaround! Really appreciate it. Completely agree this is a temporary solution — hopefully SAP addresses this natively within Datasphere soon...

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales Cloud: Q2 2026 Innovation Highlights
entry.content:
@sconway thanks for sharing this in Advance. Already looking forward to participate also in Live session.

But I already have a question... is "Offline SAP Retail Execution App" feature part of the usual SAP Sales Cloud app or the user will need to download an additional app for retail execution?

Thanks Again!
Saurabh

entry.title: Personal Settings in ME21N etc. (Tab. ESDUS) empty after conversion
entry.content:
Hello,

Our R/3 system has been converted to an S/4HANA sandbox system.
Unfortunately, the personal settings for MM transactions, which are stored in table ESDUS, are no longer available after the conversion.
I could not find any documentation stating that this is standard behavior.

Is there a standard SAP procedure, report, or migration tool available to transfer or restore ESDUS entries during or after an S/4HANA system conversion?

Many thanks!

entry.title: RAP managed early internal numbering and static factory action - retrieve automatic guid
entry.content:
Dear Commuity,

I am currently diving right into RAP for a certain usecase I want to build completely in RAP.

Usecase:
Business Object A: holds some data
Business Object B: is created based on the data in Business Object A. Business Object B has a header and a 1:1 sub entity. Latter has the exact same key as the header (1:1 relationsship).

My technical concept:
Create a static factory action in Business Object B, which:

  1. creates the header entity
  2. creates the sub entity
  3. fills the sub entity (in my case XML-message) based on the values from Business Object A.

As I want my callers to know as low as possible of the internals of Business Object B I used managed internal/automatic (and therefor implicitly early) numbering with a key field of type uuid.

Problem:
I need this exact uuid to put it in the XML message created and stored in the sub entity.
But I did not find a way of accessing this. The IMPORTING parameters of the static factory action contains only the %cid and a custom parameter (%param).

The two working solutions are not ideal:

  1. creating the instances blank and modify the content later --> unneccessary knowledge needed at the user about what methods needed to be called one after another.
  2. Supply the numbering when creating the instances --> unneccessary UUID-creation at the caller which could be done by RAP itself.

 

Does anyone have an idea how to access the GUIDs created by the RAP framework inside a static factory action?

Thanks and best regards
Michael

entry.title: Re: Posting period Queue error in GR reversal
entry.content: Will the same solution applicable for the PGI?? We would like to post PGI with back date posting.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors AI - AI based Skill-Matching in Recruiting (Set up and hints)
entry.content:
Hi @jenny_geipel,

We've explored the Skill matching feature a bit and did a few out-of-the-box tests.

No one would probably add in the CV that he/she is not good in something but the AI feature doesn't get the context - seems like its a match on words.

In my screenshot below you see that there is the statement "I'm not good in payroll analysis" but its matched as Payroll Management skill.

Funny, isn't it? 🙂

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 2 — Roll & Move
entry.content:
1.

const cds = require('@sap/cds')

module.exports = class GameService extends cds.ApplicationService {
  async init() {

    const { Players, BoardSquares } = this.entities

    // ── startTurn ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Waiting]  →  CAP validates status before this runs
    // @TO: #Playing      →  CAP updates status after this returns
    // No business logic needed — CAP's @flow.status handles the transition.
    this.on('startTurn', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Intentionally empty: the @from/@to annotations do all the work.
    })

    // ── rollDice ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Playing]  →  CAP validates status before this runs
    // @TO: #Moving       →  CAP updates status after this returns
    this.on('rollDice', 'Players', async (req) => {
      const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

      const roll = Math.ceil(Math.random() * 6)
      let newPosition = player.position + roll

      // Can't overshoot square 100 — must land exactly
      if (newPosition > 100) {
        await UPDATE(Players).set({ lastRoll: roll }).where({ ID: player.ID })
        const needed = 100 - player.position
        return req.reject(409, `Need exactly ${needed} to finish. Rolled ${roll}. No move.`)
      }

      // Resolve snake or ladder on the new square
      const square = await SELECT.one.from(BoardSquares).where({ square: newPosition })
      let finalPosition = newPosition
      let event = null

      if (square?.ladderTo) {
        finalPosition = square.ladderTo
        event = { type: 'ladder', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
      } else if (square?.snakeTo) {
        finalPosition = square.snakeTo
        event = square.isDoubleHead
          ? { type: 'doubleSnake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
          : { type: 'snake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
      }

      // Persist position and last roll
      await UPDATE(Players)
        .set({ position: finalPosition, lastRoll: roll })
        .where({ ID: player.ID })

      // Emit domain event so subscribers (Week 3) can react
      if (event) await this.emit('BoardEvent', { playerID: player.ID, ...event })

      // Use req.reply() — not return — to pin the response value before CAP's
      // @flow.status after-handler runs. A plain `return` gets cleared by the
      // flow machinery, producing HTTP 204 instead of the expected 200 + body.
      req.reply({ roll, position: finalPosition, event: event?.type ?? 'normal' })
    })

    // ── confirmMove ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  normal end of a turn, hands off to next player (Week 3)
    // @TO: #Waiting     →  player waits for their next turn
    this.on('confirmMove', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Week 3 will add turn rotation here.
      // For now: just let CAP apply @TO: #Waiting
    })

    // ── blockPlayer ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  called when player lands on a double-headed snake
    // @TO: #Blocked     →  player skips their next turn
    this.on('blockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Business logic: the position was already updated in rollDice.
      // This action just transitions state — CAP handles the @TO: #Blocked update.
      req.info('Player is blocked and will skip their next turn.')
    })

    // ── unblockPlayer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Blocked]   →  called at the start of a blocked player's next turn
    // @TO: $flow.previous →  CAP restores the status to whatever it was before #Blocked
    this.on('unblockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // CAP's $flow.previous does the work — no code needed here.
      // The player's status returns to #Waiting automatically.
    })

    // ── winGame ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  called when player.position === 100
    // @TO: #Finished    →  player has won
    this.on('winGame', 'Players', async (req) => {
      const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

      // Mark the session as finished with this player as winner
      await UPDATE('snakeladder.GameSessions')
        .set({
          finishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
          winner_ID: player.ID
        })
        .where({ ID: player.session_ID })

      await this.emit('GameWon', { playerID: player.ID, sessionID: player.session_ID })
    })

    return super.init()
  }
}

2.

3.

4. It seems to allow the execution of winGame as long as the player is in the Moving status, even if the player is not at position 100. However, I did not see that the game session got updated with the winner, until I realized that the player I created was not associated to a session. Once I gave the player a session, it updated the session.

But I added a check and now I cannot do that.

Stretch.

All great, though I had to add that field to the entity definition. I didn't really understand what the use in the game was for turnsBlocked -- just a total counter of the total times that player was blocked? 

---------------------------------------

Note I had to fix the module type in package.json before it would work as expected (at first I simply renamed the file cjs but this only let the service run, but roll dice did not work)

From Claude:

Done. Removed "type": "module" from package.json so .js files default back to CommonJS, and renamed srv/game-service.js back. The file content is unchanged — it was already valid CommonJS.

entry.title: Re: Processing Electronic Invoices in France with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content: @AdilHoussa if you are submitting B2B invoices through PA, please note that is incorrect process (this is for B2G). E-doc should be created belong Domestic customer invoices. Please refer to SAP Help (SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition). I would recommend to watch webinar reply and use country-specific workzone for further exchange: https://workzone.one.int.sap/site#workzone-home&/groups/CK8AhwChqYjMisy8oYSfgv/workpage_tabs/LxnjcOht6DIhuitgncZLhH.

entry.title: Re: Processing Electronic Invoices in France with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
@MaeW  Yes. Electronic Invoicing via Peppol is former solution still required for B2G. Electronic Invoicing and Reporting via Approved Platform is the latest solution, for the mandate becoming effective in September. Also in this case, the invoice is sent to recipient through Peppol network.

You should follow Electronic Invoicing and Reporting via Approved Platform.

entry.title: The total balance of offsetting account after data migration is not zero.
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

According to the posting logic during migration, the total balance of each migration clearing account or offsetting account, which is used for the offsetting postings, must be zero..

However, the data migration has already been completed, and I am now observing that the closing balances of the offsetting accounts are not zero across all company codes.

Has anyone encountered a similar issue or have any idea what might be causing this?

Best regards,
Emal Rahman

entry.title: Re: How to show a comparison of price lists or last price...
entry.content:
Yes, this functionality is already available in standard SAP Business One.

When you are creating a Purchase Order, place the cursor in the Unit Price field and press Ctrl + Tab. This opens the Last Prices Report, where you can review the item's purchase price history.

The report allows you to:

  • View prices from different vendors.
  • See prices from previous purchasing documents (Purchase Orders, Goods Receipt POs, A/P Invoices, etc., depending on your settings and available history).
  • Compare historical purchase prices before selecting the appropriate one.

As far as I know, there is no standard option to add a "Price List" entry to the right-click context menu of the Unit Price field. If you specifically need that behavior, it will require a customization (for example, using the SAP Business One UI API or an add-on).

For most purchasing scenarios, however, Ctrl + Tab in the Unit Price field is the standard SAP Business One way to access the Last Prices Report.

entry.content:
Hi @apoonia02 , thanks for sharing this, really helpful.
Since we have FES deployment of Fiori, would enabling Fiori as a SaaS application in BTP also result in the same outcome for S/4HANA Public Cloud?
I'm sure exposing Fiori CDM data is not equivalent to enabling Fiori as a SaaS application.

BR,
Denzil

entry.title: Re: PR Workflow Issue
entry.content:
Hi @watertec ,

SAP has not added document type in the workflow, nor has it been placed on the roadmap.  The good news is that you can add it with a little bit of code and the extension points below.

Extension Points to Insert Code:

  1. SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF - using this extension point, you could create a new condition, simple, name such as "Requisition Document Type"
  2. SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL - using this extension point, you would evaluate it and import CDS view(s) released for Key User Extensibility - such as I_PURCHASEREQUISITIONAPI01 / Document type

Then, use the standard flexible workflow definition apps - namely Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions. The new condition will appear as part of #1.  Then it will assess based upon #2

Please reward points and deem as "Answered" if this helped! 😃

entry.title: Missing descriptions when downloading the service catalog
entry.content:
Hello,

We have noticed that when downloading the service catalog in SAP for ME, the descriptions are missing. The entries are included in the download, but the corresponding description texts are not.

Any idea?

Thank you and best regards,
Frank

entry.title: Re: HR Portal-To Be or Not To Be
entry.content: Hi @AviadRivlin  : Please share the new SCN and Twitter link.

entry.title: Re: Best Practice / Legal Requirements for Retention of C...
entry.content: Relevant blog on the How To for this topic in SuccessFactors: https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/overview-of-all-data-purge-options-and-compensation-business-case-drtm/ba-p/13539835

entry.title: From Classical SAP to AI Architect: Solving Business Bottlenecks with Gen AI and the BTP Framework
entry.content:
Join us for an exciting SAP Community Nigeria session: "From Classical SAP to AI Architect: Solving Business Bottlenecks with Generative AI and the Adaptive Semantic Extraction Framework on SAP BTP for S/4HANA."

Whether you're a classical SAP professional just starting your AI journey or looking to deepen your BTP knowledge, this session is for you. We'll break down what AI and BTP mean for classical modules and how you can begin pivoting your skills into this space - followed by a real-world success story of how the Adaptive Semantic Extraction Framework was used to automate complex sales orders and solve major operational bottlenecks.

Date: 22nd July 2026 | Time: 3PM WAT | Location: Virtual

Reserve your spot here- https://bit.ly/4fipnc1

SAP Community 

@SAP
